examples.title: HTTP GET Request
examples.description: Demonstrates structure of a typical HTTP GET request
examples.code:
// HTTP GET Request Format
GET /index.html HTTP/1.1
Host: www.example.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml
Accept-Language: en-US,en;q=0.9
Connection: keep-alive

// Empty body for GET requests
examples.explanation: A GET request includes the method, resource path, HTTP version, followed by headers. The Host header is mandatory in HTTP/1.1. GET requests typically have no body.

examples.title: HTTP POST Request
examples.description: Demonstrates structure of a typical HTTP POST request with form data
examples.code:
// HTTP POST Request Format
POST /submit-form HTTP/1.1
Host: www.example.com
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 43
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0
Accept: text/html

// Request body contains data
username=john&email=john@example.com
examples.explanation: POST requests include a body with data to be submitted to the server. Content-Type header specifies the format of the data being sent.

examples.title: HTTP Response
examples.description: Demonstrates structure of an HTTP success response
examples.code:
// HTTP Response Format HTTP/1.1 200 OK Date: Mon, 27 Jul 2024 12:28:53 GMT Server: Apache/2.4.41 Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Content-Length: 1245 Cache-Control: max-age=3600 // Response body (HTML content) Example

Welcome
examples.explanation: HTTP response includes status line with version and status code, followed by headers and optional body containing the requested resource.

examples.title: Simple Web Server (Pseudo-code)
examples.description: Basic web server process using HTTP
examples.code:
BEGIN WebServer
 serverSocket = CreateSocket(8080)
 WHILE true DO
 clientSocket = serverSocket.Accept()
 request = clientSocket.Receive()
 
 // Parse HTTP request
 method = ParseMethod(request)
 path = ParsePath(request)
 headers = ParseHeaders(request)
 
 // Route request
 IF method = "GET" AND path = "/index.html" THEN
 response = "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\nContent-Type: text/html\r\n\r\n<html>..."
 ELSE IF method = "GET" AND path = "/404" THEN
 response = "HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found\r\nContent-Type: text/html\r\n\r\n404 Not Found"
 ELSE
 response = "HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error\r\n\r\n"
 END IF
 
 clientSocket.Send(response)
 clientSocket.Close()
 END WHILE
END WebServer
examples.explanation: This pseudo-code shows the basic flow of a web server: listening for connections, accepting client requests, parsing HTTP, and sending appropriate responses with status codes.

examples.title: HTTP Caching Logic
examples.description: Demonstrates conditional GET and caching mechanism
examples.code:
BEGIN ClientRequest(url)
 // Check browser cache
 cachedResponse = GetFromCache(url)
 
 IF cachedResponse IS NOT NULL AND NOT Expired(cachedResponse) THEN
 RETURN cachedResponse // Use cached version
 ELSE
 // Need to validate or fetch fresh copy
 request = CreateGETRequest(url)
 
 IF cachedResponse IS NOT NULL THEN
 // Add validation headers
 eTag = cachedResponse.GetHeader("ETag")
 lastModified = cachedResponse.GetHeader("Last-Modified")
 AddHeader(request, "If-None-Match", eTag)
 AddHeader(request, "If-Modified-Since", lastModified)
 END IF
 
 response = SendRequest(request)
 
 IF response.status = 304 THEN
 // Not modified - use cached version
 UpdateCacheExpiry(cachedResponse)
 RETURN cachedResponse
 ELSE
 // New response - cache it
 AddToCache(url, response)
 RETURN response
 END IF
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates conditional GET using ETag and Last-Modified headers for cache validation, reducing bandwidth by returning 304 Not Modified when cached content is still valid.
